JScience 1.0.0-SNAPSHOT
  1. org.jscience.social.arts.music

Uses of Package
org.jscience.social.arts.music

Packages that use org.jscience.social.arts.music
Package
Description
org.jscience.social.arts.loaders
Core functionality for the org.jscience.social.arts.loaders package in the JScience library.
org.jscience.social.arts.music
- musical notes, instruments, compositions.
org.jscience.social.arts.theater
- plays, performances, dramatic arts.
  • Classes in org.jscience.social.arts.music used by org.jscience.social.arts.loaders
    Class
    Description
    Composition
    Represents a musical composition with multiple tracks and signatures.
    Measure
    Represents a musical measure (bar).
    Note
    Represents a musical note with microtonal and dynamic properties.
    Part
    Represents a part for a single instrument in a musical score.
    Score
    Represents a musical score (notation).
  • Classes in org.jscience.social.arts.music used by org.jscience.social.arts.music
    Class
    Description
    CounterpointChecker.Violation
     
    Duration
    Represents a musical duration.
    Dynamics
    Represents musical dynamics.
    HarmonyEngine.ScaleType
     
    Instrument
    Represents a musical instrument.
    Instrument.Family
     
    Instrument.Type
     
    InstrumentationAssistant.Instrument
     
    KeySignature
    Represents a musical key signature.
    Measure
    Represents a musical measure (bar).
    MusicalFormAnalyzer.MusicalForm
     
    MusicalFormAnalyzer.Section
     
    Note
    Represents a musical note with microtonal and dynamic properties.
    OrchestrationSolver.OrchestralBalance
     
    Part
    Represents a part for a single instrument in a musical score.
    Pitch
    Represents a musical pitch.
    RhythmEngine.RhythmCell
    A rhythmic cell represented as note durations in subdivisions of a beat.
    RhythmEngine.RhythmStyle
     
    TimeSignature
    Represents a musical time signature (e.g., 4/4).
    Track
    A single track in a musical composition, typically for one instrument.

    Composition
